events.text: Donald Young, 66-year-old Asian male, Former smoker (quit 2 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the Sigmoid colon. Clinical staging: T3N0M0, AJCC Stage IIA. ICD-10: C18.9. Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent surgical resection: Laparoscopic left hemicolectomy with lymph node dissection (22 nodes examined, 0/22 positive). Estimated blood loss 300 mL. Length of stay 5 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 7. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 8. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 8 assessment.
Developed grade 2 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after adjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after adjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Donald Young
MRN: patient_d713b321e420
Date of Study: 2022-09-07

CLINICAL INDICATION: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Findings consistent with moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma as described.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Donald Young
MRN: patient_d713b321e420
Specimen ID: S2022-45091
Date of Procedure: 2022-09-09
Date of Report: 2022-09-13

SPECIMEN: Sigmoid colon, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.9 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CDX2: Positive
CK20: Positive
CK7: Negative
MLH1: Intact
MSH2: Intact
MSH6: Intact
PMS2: Intact

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Donald Young - Sigmoid colon" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 3.9 x 2.7 x 1.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Donald Young
MRN: patient_d713b321e420
Date: 2022-09-13
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Young is a 66-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, IIA. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy. Pathology confirmed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports incidental finding on routine screening.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- gastroesophageal reflux disease
- obstructive sleep apnea
- coronary artery disease

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: Former heavy drinker, quit 5 years ago
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No significant family history.

ALLERGIES: Penicillin (rash)

MEDICATIONS:
- Omeprazole 20 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild cough, night sweats, intermittent nausea. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 120/76, HR 82, RR 16, Temp 98.5F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 57.7 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 7.2, Hgb 11.6, Plt 120, Cr 1.0

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
66-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. initial oncology consultation.

1. Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, Stage IIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend surgical resection
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Richard Okafor, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Donald Young
MRN: patient_d713b321e420
Date of Report: 2022-09-19
Specimen: Sigmoid colon (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma
Panel: Tempus xT (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.9 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS Wild-type - No mutation [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  APC p.R1450* (c.4348C>T) - Nonsense (Exon 16)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  SMAD4 p.R361C - VUS
  FBXW7 p.R465H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS wild-type: Patient eligible for anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab) if disease progresses.
  - MSS: Unlikely to benefit from single-agent immune checkpoint inhibitor.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 796x coverage.
Tumor content: 55%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Donald Young
MRN: patient_d713b321e420
Specimen ID: S2022-72250
Date of Procedure: 2022-10-12
Date of Report: 2022-10-14

SPECIMEN: Sigmoid colon, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 1.8 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CDX2: Positive
CK20: Positive
CK7: Negative
MLH1: Intact
MSH2: Intact
MSH6: Intact
PMS2: Intact

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Donald Young - Sigmoid colon" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 1.8 x 1.3 x 0.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Patient: Donald Young
MRN: patient_d713b321e420
Date: 2022-10-23
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Young is a 66-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient is tolerating treatment reasonably well. Appetite has improved.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: intermittent headaches, unintentional weight loss, intermittent nausea. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 127/75, HR 79, RR 16, Temp 98.5F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 60.3 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 5.9, Hgb 10.3, Plt 233, Cr 0.75

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
66-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. postoperative follow-up.

1. Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma - postoperative follow-up
   - Proceed with next cycle as planned
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Continue antiemetic regimen
3. Follow-up in 4 weeks

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Findings consistent with moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma as described.
2. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

Department of Radiology

scenario_blurb: Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy
